name: The Needleman Law Office
description_short: The Needleman Law Office is a Columbus-based bankruptcy law firm with 30+ years of experience helping individuals and small businesses file Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy throughout Ohio.
description_long:
The Needleman Law Office has been operating for over 30 years and specializes exclusively in bankruptcy law for individuals and small businesses across Ohio, primarily serving the Southern District including Central Ohio, Franklin, Delaware, and Knox counties. The firm positions itself as a direct-attorney service rather than a paralegal-dependent operation, emphasizing that clients work with an actual bankruptcy attorney during consultations and throughout their cases.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy, Chapter 13 repayment plan bankruptcy, debt settlement and negotiation, foreclosure prevention through bankruptcy filing, and specialized student loan bankruptcy guidance. They also provide free virtual debt relief consultations lasting 15 minutes or less, which is a defined marketing promise on their website. Their service model includes a transparent three-step process: initial no-cost telephone consultation, paperwork preparation with credit counseling instructions, and final filing with court hearing preparation.

What distinguishes The Needleman Law Office is their emphasis on direct attorney involvement, their flat-fee pricing structure (quoted during consultation based on individual circumstances), and their explicit promise of dignified treatment during difficult financial situations. The firm also offers mock bankruptcy hearing practice to prepare clients for their actual 341 hearing, and they provide follow-up email summaries after initial consultations to ensure transparency.

As a law firm rather than a debt relief or settlement company, their service is fundamentally different from consolidation or settlement agencies. The main caveat is that bankruptcy is presented as a last resort, and they acknowledge during consultations they may recommend alternative routes depending on client circumstances. Specific financial outcomes (credit score recovery, discharge amounts) are not guaranteed on their website, and Chapter 7 cases are noted as typically being "no asset cases," meaning limited asset recovery.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 30+ years of bankruptcy-specific experience with direct attorney consultation (not paralegal-dependent)
Free virtual debt relief consultations with defined 15-minute timeframe
Flat-fee pricing structure transparent during initial consultation
Serves entire Southern District of Ohio including multiple counties (Franklin, Delaware, Knox)
Offers mock 341 hearing practice to prepare clients before actual bankruptcy court hearing
Explicitly stops creditor calls even before filing (via attorney contact information)
Transparent three-step process with email summaries after consultations
Addresses multiple bankruptcy scenarios (Chapter 7, Chapter 13, foreclosure prevention, student loans)
cons: Bankruptcy is presented as a 'last resort,' and attorney may recommend alternative solutions, creating potential for redirected clients
Chapter 7 cases typically result in 'no asset cases,' meaning limited financial recovery for some clients
No specific information provided about success rates, average discharge amounts, or credit score recovery timelines
Student loan bankruptcy help described as finding 'alternative solutions' rather than discharge, limiting value for student loan debtors
Flat-fee pricing not disclosed upfront on website; requires consultation to receive quote
best_for: Columbus and Central Ohio residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy decisions
Small business owners in Ohio needing to file business bankruptcy alongside personal debt
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking temporary halt through bankruptcy automatic stay
Individuals overwhelmed by medical debt, credit card debt, or personal loans seeking dignified legal guidance
diagnosis: Best for Central Ohio residents and small business owners requiring Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing who want direct attorney involvement and transparent flat-fee pricing. Main caveat: bankruptcy is positioned as a last resort, and the attorney may recommend alternative solutions during the free consultation, so this is a consultation-based filtering service as much as a guaranteed filing service.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and asset liquidation
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and repayment plan creation
Foreclosure prevention and temporary halt through bankruptcy filing
Student loan bankruptcy alternative solutions and guidance
Automatic Stay initiation to stop creditor collection activities
Mock 341 hearing (creditor meeting) preparation and practice
Flat-fee bankruptcy representation for individuals and small businesses
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
